title: amygdalus hybrida
intro: Amygdalus hybrida, also known as the apricot kernel tree, is a deciduous tree native to Central Asia. The kernel of the apricot fruit contains a compound called amygdalin, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer effects. The kernel has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Amygdalus hybrida is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and oil extracts, to support overall health and well-being.
